role: Founder of the Worker-communist Party of Iran
bio: Mansoor Hekmat (1951–2002), born Zhoobin Razani, was an Iranian Marxist revolutionary and the founder of the worker-communist movement. After the [1979 revolution](/events/iranian-revolution-1979), he established the movement of Revolutionary Marxism and in 1983 co-founded the [Communist Party of Iran](/orgs/communist-party-of-iran) together with the [Komala](/orgs/komala-party-of-iranian-kurdistan) movement. In 1991 he broke away to found the [Worker-communist Party of Iran](/orgs/worker-communist-party-of-iran), rejecting both the Soviet model and Third World nationalism in favour of a humanist Marxism centred on working-class self-emancipation. Hekmat was a prolific writer and theorist whose works criticised nationalism, political Islam, and identity politics from a universalist perspective. He led the WPI from exile until his death from cancer on 4 July 2002 in London. His death triggered a leadership crisis that split the party into rival factions.
editorial_notes: Born Zhoobin Razani. Major Iranian Marxist theorist. Died in London 2002.
